macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 Posts Fifth Consecutive Weekly Loss Amid Middle East Tensions
items.summary: The S&P 500 fell 2% last week, marking its first five-week losing streak since 2022, as markets grapple with Middle East conflict uncertainty and elevated oil prices pushing Brent crude to $112 per barrel.

items.title: U.S. Treasury Yields Rise to 4.43% as Inflation Concerns Mount
items.summary: U.S. 10-year Treasury yields climbed to 4.43% amid elevated oil prices and inflation concerns, shifting the central bank debate from potential rate cuts to whether policy rates can keep pace with rising inflation.

items.title: Norway's Norges Bank Hikes Policy Rate in May, Plans Additional Increase
items.summary: Norway's central bank raised its policy rate in May and is expected to implement one more rate hike in 2026 as inflation remains above target, driven by higher oil and commodity prices.

items.title: AI-Driven Power Demand and Middle East Supply Shock Unlock Energy Infrastructure Investment
items.summary: BlackRock identifies dual forces—AI-driven power demand and Middle East conflict-driven energy security concerns—creating thematic investment opportunities across energy, infrastructure, and commodities sectors.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Circle Secures $222 Million Presale for Arc Blockchain
items.summary: Stablecoin powerhouse Circle Internet Group raised $222 million in a token presale for its Arc blockchain, achieving a $3 billion valuation with backing from Andreessen Horowitz, BlackRock, and Apollo Funds. The layer-1 blockchain emphasizes regulatory compliance and privacy using zero-knowledge proofs.

items.title: Senate Preps for Clarity Act Vote on Cryptocurrency Regulation
items.summary: The Senate is preparing to vote on the Clarity Act, marking a significant regulatory development for the cryptocurrency industry.

items.title: Crypto Market Experiences $410 Million in Liquidations
items.summary: The cryptocurrency market saw approximately $410 million in liquidations over 24 hours, with $165 million in long positions and $240 million in short positions liquidated due to Bitcoin price volatility between $80,000-$82,000.

items.title: Solana Gains 15% on $39 Million ETF Inflows
items.summary: Solana (SOL) surged 15% to $97 following $39 million in weekly spot ETF inflows, demonstrating strong institutional interest in the blockchain platform.

items.title: Bitcoin Consolidates Near $81,000 as Major Altcoins Face Selling Pressure
items.summary: Bitcoin remains range-bound below $84,000 while Ethereum, Solana, Binance Coin, and Ripple experience selling pressure in a broad market pullback.

items.title: $737.7 Million in Token Unlocks Expected During Second Week of May 2026
items.summary: The crypto market faces significant token unlocks totaling $737.7 million this week, with Connex, Avalanche, and Arbitrum leading the releases.

items.title: Hedera (HBAR) Climbs to $0.097 Signaling Potential Breakout
items.summary: Hedera Hashgraph's native token HBAR reached $0.097 with a $4.2 billion market cap, indicating a potential recovery from its long-term downtrend.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Meta Hires Four More OpenAI Researchers
items.summary: Meta has reportedly hired four additional researchers from OpenAI: Shengjia Zhao, Jiahui Yu, Shuchao Bi, and Hongyu Ren, according to a person familiar with the hires.

items.title: OpenAI's ChatGPT Experiences Downtime
items.summary: Users are reporting issues with ChatGPT, including problems loading prompts, leading to widespread outages.

items.title: Apple to Open AI Technology to Developers
items.summary: Apple plans to allow developers access to its underlying AI technology, potentially expanding its ecosystem.

items.title: US-UAE AI Data Campus Deal Not Finalized
items.summary: A multi-billion dollar deal between the US and UAE for an AI data campus remains far from completion, sources indicate.

items.title: ITER Develops Fusion World Model with AI
items.summary: ITER is training a 'fusion world model' using AI on a GPU cluster to simulate tokamak behavior, predict faults, and transfer knowledge from existing machines for improved operations.

items.title: 68% of Students Use AI for Math Help
items.summary: A new survey reveals 68% of students rely on AI tools for math assignments or exams, with 10% using them daily, highlighting AI's growing role in education.

items.title: OpenAI Launches AI Consulting Arm Valued at $14 Billion
items.summary: OpenAI has launched a new AI consulting division valued at $14 billion, expanding its services beyond core model development.

items.title: AI Learning Gap Raises Investor Concerns
items.summary: Investors express worries over gaps in AI learning capabilities, potentially impacting development and adoption paces.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Jeff Clarke's Mission to Save Dell PCs
items.summary: Jeff Clarke is taking over Dell's PC business to reverse years of drift, restore the XPS halo, and compete in an AI era where fundamentals still win.

items.title: AI PCs' Unmet Promise Dragging Down Adoption
items.summary: AI PCs were marketed as transformative, but confusing messaging, underpowered hardware, and a lack of compelling use cases have left buyers unconvinced.

items.title: Percona's Fast-Impact Database Services Aim to Speed AI Readiness
items.summary: Percona is refocusing on fast, structured database services to help enterprises overcome talent shortages, improve performance, and prepare open-source data platforms for AI readiness.

items.title: The Android PC: Google's Second Shot at the Unified Computing Dream
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are pushing Android PCs into the mainstream, aiming to reshape personal computing by scaling the dominant mobile OS to desktops and challenging Microsoft and Apple.

items.title: Silicon Computing Poised To Make Quantum Leap
items.summary: Quantum Motion has deployed the first full-stack quantum computer built using standard silicon chip fabrication, a milestone that could accelerate scalable quantum systems.
